To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix. The characteristic polynomial is given by $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{array}\right) $$

To simplify the calculation, let's clear the denominators. The least common multiple of the denominators (16, 4, 8) is 16. We can define a new matrix $B = 16A$. The eigenvalues of $B$ will be $16$ times the eigenvalues of $A$. If the characteristic polynomial of $B$ is $q(\mu) = \det(B - \mu I)$, then the characteristic polynomial of $A$ is related by a scaling factor. Specifically, if $\mu = 16\lambda$, then:
$$ \det(B - \mu I) = \det(16A - 16\lambda I) = \det(16(A - \lambda I)) = 16^3 \det(A - \lambda I) = 4096 p(\lambda) $$
So, $p(\lambda) = \frac{1}{4096} q(16\lambda)$.

Let's compute the matrix $B = 16A$:
$$ B = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
